BEAVER HEALTHCARE AND REHABILITATION CENTER

CCN: 395109 · ALIQUIPPA, PA 15001 · Beaver County

Overview

Address
616 GOLF COURSE ROAD, ALIQUIPPA, PA 15001
Phone
7243750345
Certified beds
67
Avg daily residents
51 (76% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1967-01-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-30 21 19 4 156 1 156
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-05 42 25 24 531 1 531
Total weighted health score
249.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (74)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

71 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. D 2025-12-03 2026-01-06
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0620 Not require residents to give up Medicare or Medicaid benefits, or pay privately as a condition of admission; and must tell residents what care they do not provide. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. E 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-05-30 2025-07-16
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-03-26 2025-04-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-09-04 2024-09-30
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-09-04 2024-09-30
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2024-09-04 2024-09-30
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-23
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-01
0620 Not require residents to give up Medicare or Medicaid benefits, or pay privately as a condition of admission; and must tell residents what care they do not provide. D 2024-06-06 2024-07-01
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. J 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. F 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-05 2024-04-19
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2024-03-05 2024-04-19

Showing 50 most recent of 74. See the All Data CSV for the full list.

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
